title: .conf file is not picked up from `%APPDATA%\containers\containers.conf.d` folder on Windows
body:
### Issue Description

`%APPDATA%\containers\` resolves to `C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Roaming\containers` on my machine.

devcontainers often have file permission issues on my machine which I have to work around with
```json
"runArgs": [
    "--userns=keep-id"
],
```

In order to keep my `devcontainer.json` files independent of the container engine (compatibility with docker), I want to put that in a separate podman configuration file.

I created `%APPDATA%\containers\containers.conf.d\1-userns-keep-id.conf` with the following content:
```toml
[containers]
userns = "keep-id"
```

After restarting the podman machine, my devcontainers still only work properly with the runArgs in the `devcontainer.json` file - it does not seem to pick up the setting from the .conf file.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create `%APPDATA%\containers\containers.conf.d\1-userns-keep-id.conf`
2. Restart podman machine
3. Run devcontainer from repository cloned in windows filesystem


### Describe the results you received

devcontainers do not work properly (without file ownership issues) without the runArgs in the `devcontainer.json` file - it does not seem to pick up the setting from the .conf file.

### Describe the results you expected

devcontainers work properly without the `runArgs` in the `devcontainer.json` file

comments.body:
> Version: 5.7.0

you machine is to old, you must remove and recreate the podman machine. The mounting of the config dir is only done for new machines starting with 6, note the orignal logic was buggy so 6.0.1 is really needed there which you have as client so that is fine.
